_readme: Each scenario is a self-contained test. 'turns' is the multi-turn conversation. 'expect' defines what the grader checks in the final agent reply. Chain scenarios share a 'session_id' and are run in order — the full history is passed to each turn.

scenarios.name: Vague intent — open browse

turns.content: I want to get something nice for my mum's birthday next week

expect.notes: Agent should ask at most ONE clarifying question OR search a sensible default. Must not dump a raw product list. Must not ask multiple questions.

scenarios.name: Specific product search — Toblerone

turns.content: Do you have Toblerone?

expect.notes: Agent must search and surface product with price. Must not just say yes/no.

scenarios.name: Filtered browse — cakes under 3000 same-day Colombo 3

turns.content: Show me cakes under 3000 rupees that can be delivered today in Colombo 3

expect.notes: Must use occasionId: cakes with budget filter. Must check delivery availability.

scenarios.name: Delegate mode — girlfriend, too busy

turns.content: girlfriend, too busy, just come up with something

must_not_contain: Let me know what you think
expect.notes: ZERO questions before searchGifts. Must search romance immediately. Forbidden phrases must not appear.

scenarios.name: Relationship repair — she's mad at me

turns.content: she's mad at me, I messed up

must_not_contain: Let me know what you think
expect.notes: Must open with friend empathy (not consultant-speak), then immediately search romance/flowers. No jewelry upsell.

scenarios.name: Diaspora flow — London sender, Kandy recipient

turns.content: I'm in London. I want to send flowers to my sister in Kandy for her anniversary tomorrow.

expect.notes: Must check Kandy is serviceable. Must ask for address and message card. Should mention LKR pricing.

scenarios.name: Emotional diaspora — haven't been home in 3 years

turns.content: It's my parents' 40th anniversary and I haven't been home in 3 years. I want to do something really special.

expect.notes: CRITICAL TEST. Agent must acknowledge the emotional moment before pushing products. Must not immediately dump a product list. Should suggest a premium combo with personal card.

scenarios.name: Urgent same-day — wife birthday today

turns.content: Oh no I completely forgot my wife's birthday is TODAY. I'm in Colombo. What can I deliver to her office in Colombo 7 right now?

expect.notes: Urgency must be acknowledged. Must check same-day eligibility for Colombo 7. Must surface time cutoff if relevant.

scenarios.name: Add to cart — explicit confirmation

turns.content: need tulips for my girlfriend asap
[PLACEHOLDER — previous agent turn]
lets add the one with pink roses

expect.notes: CRITICAL. Must call addToCart. Must NOT say 'Shall we proceed to checkout?' Must offer cross-sell (chocolates or card) before checkout prompt.

scenarios.name: No premature cart add — preference expressed, not confirmed

turns.content: she loves KitKat chocolates

expect.notes: CRITICAL. 'She loves X' is NOT add-to-cart permission. Must search, not add.

scenarios.name: Context retention across 5 turns

turns.content: I live in Nugegoda.
[PLACEHOLDER]
My name is Roshan.
[PLACEHOLDER]
I want to buy a birthday cake.
[PLACEHOLDER]
For my daughter. She likes strawberry.
[PLACEHOLDER]
What do you recommend?

expect.notes: CRITICAL MEMORY TEST. Final response must use ALL context — Nugegoda, Roshan, daughter, strawberry — without re-asking anything.

scenarios.name: Mode switch — shopping to order tracking

turns.content: Find me some nice chocolates.
[PLACEHOLDER]
Wait, I have a complaint about a previous order first. Order KP-2024-88712 arrived damaged.

expect.notes: Must switch to TRACK mode. Must acknowledge complaint with empathy FIRST. Must call trackOrder.

scenarios.name: Mode switch — back to chat after tracking

turns.content: Find me some nice chocolates.
[PLACEHOLDER]
Wait, I have a complaint about a previous order first. Order KP-2024-88712.
[MODE: TRACK] Let me pull that up right away. [trackOrder called]
OK sorted. Back to the chocolates.

expect.notes: Must switch back to CHAT. Must include a one-sentence bridge summarising the order outcome. Must NOT ask what they wanted — agent remembers chocolates.

scenarios.name: Sinhala-English code switching

turns.content: Api kawda, I want to send a gift to my amme in Galle. She likes flowers meka wage.

expect.notes: Must understand Sinhala-English naturally. Must not refuse or ask them to switch language. Must respond helpfully.

scenarios.name: Out of stock — honest redirect

turns.content: I want the Black Forest cake for tomorrow delivery in Jaffna.

expect.notes: If unavailable — must offer alternatives honestly. Must check delivery to Jaffna.

scenarios.name: Tight budget — 1500 rupees

turns.content: I only have 1500 rupees. What's the best gift I can give?

expect.notes: Must work within the stated budget. Must not suggest things over budget. Must call optimizeBudget or searchGifts with maxPrice 1500.

scenarios.name: Competitor mention — price match

turns.content: Daraz has this cheaper. Can you match the price?

expect.notes: Must not badmouth Daraz. Must not make promises about price matching it cannot fulfil. Must highlight Kapruka's genuine value proposition.

scenarios.name: Angry customer — third late order

turns.content: This is the THIRD time my order has been late. I'm done with Kapruka. Give me a refund NOW.

expect.notes: CRITICAL. Must de-escalate FIRST. Must not defend Kapruka or lecture policy. Must acknowledge 'third time' specifically. Must route to support.

scenarios.name: Out of scope — flight booking

turns.content: Can you book me a flight to Kandy?

expect.notes: Must politely decline flights. Must immediately bridge to what Kapruka CAN do for Kandy. Must not just say no.

scenarios.name: Full checkout — add, address, confirm

turns.content: Add 2 boxes of Milk Tray chocolates and a bunch of red roses to my cart
[PLACEHOLDER]
Deliver to 45/2 Flower Road, Colombo 7. Leave it with the security guard.
[PLACEHOLDER]
Yes, confirm the order.

expect.notes: Must carry address AND the security guard note forward. Must not re-ask for address. Must show checkout form.

scenarios.name: Mid-checkout swap — roses to mixed bouquet

turns.content: Add the pink roses to my cart
[PLACEHOLDER — cart now has pink roses]
Actually can I swap the roses for a mixed bouquet instead?

expect.notes: Must swap without restarting checkout. Must not lose the rest of the cart context.

scenarios.name: Card message offer — near checkout, romantic context

turns.content: I want to send flowers to my girlfriend for Valentine's.
[PLACEHOLDER — showed flowers]
Add the pink roses.

expect.notes: After cart add in romantic context, agent must offer card message OR chocolates. The checkout prompt alone is a FAIL.

scenarios.name: No fabrication — agent must not invent stock or prices

turns.content: Is there a Lindt Excellence 90% dark chocolate available? How much is it?

expect.notes: Agent must NOT invent a price or stock status. Must call searchGifts and report what the tool returns. If not found, must say so honestly.

scenarios.name: Sympathy flow — no upsell

turns.content: I need to send something to my aunt. Her husband just passed away.

expect.notes: CRITICAL. No cross-sell, no upsell in sympathy flow. Empathy first. No toxic positivity.

scenarios.name: Recipient-led — something for my dad, no product hint

turns.content: Hi, I want to get something for my dad.

expect.notes: CRITICAL — regression test. Relationship known, no taste info: must search FIRST (never ask a question before searching — the grader only checks that searchGifts fires; asking a personal lean-in question AFTER cards, like 'what does he like', is correct and expected), and must pick a real product vertical (chocolates/perfumes/fruit) instead of the non-existent 'father' MCP category.

scenarios.name: Recipient-led — something nice for my mum, taste unknown

turns.content: I want to get something nice for my mum, not totally sure what she'd like.

expect.notes: Occasion (mum) IS known — this is NOT Case B uncertainty even though 'not sure' appears. Must search immediately, not open the picker.

scenarios.name: Recipient-led — sister's birthday, no idea what she'd like

turns.content: It's my sister's birthday next week and I have no idea what she'd like.

expect.notes: Occasion (birthday) AND recipient (sister) are both known — 'no idea' here refers to taste, not being fully lost. Case B (picker) requires NO clear occasion/recipient at all, so this must search, not open the picker.

scenarios.name: Genuine uncertainty on the very first message

turns.content: Hey, I need to get a gift for someone but honestly I have no idea what to get. Surprise me.

expect.notes: No recipient, no occasion, no product hint — truly lost. Case B applies even on turn 1 (this is a base-prompt rule, not the server's forced-injection safety net which only kicks in from turn 2).

scenarios.name: REGRESSION — 'i dont really know' on turn 2 must open the picker

turns.content: hi i wanna get something for my dad
[PLACEHOLDER — dad gift turn]
i dont really know

expect.notes: CRITICAL REGRESSION TEST for the exact bug reported in production: 'i dont really know' was not matched by the old uncertainty regex (it only matched 'dont know' with nothing in between), so the picker never opened and the model kept guessing searches instead. This must now force showGiftFinder.

scenarios.name: Still unsure after finishing the picker — pivot, don't re-open it

turns.content: Something for my dad, I'm not sure what he'd like.
[PLACEHOLDER — dad gift turn]
Honestly I still have no idea, whatever you think is best.

expect.notes: Buyer already completed the chip picker this session (simulated via gift_finder_already_complete). Re-opening the picker would feel like a loop — must instead search a fresh occasionId directly.

scenarios.name: Dad rejects chocolates — must pivot category, not repeat it

turns.content: Something for my dad, please.
[PLACEHOLDER — dad chocolates turn]
He doesn't really like chocolates.

expect.notes: Explicit category rejection must store the dislike and pivot to a genuinely different vertical (perfumes/fruit/cakes) — not retry chocolates.

scenarios.name: Delegate mode — dad's birthday today, too busy to choose

turns.content: It's my dad's birthday today and I'm completely swamped at work — just pick something good for him.

expect.notes: Recipient (dad) AND occasion (birthday) both known + explicit delegate signal ('just pick') → ZERO questions, search immediately.

scenarios.name: Product type known wins — cologne for dad

turns.content: I want to get some cologne for my dad.

expect.notes: Product type (cologne) is explicit, so RULE A applies: occasionId must be the product vertical 'perfumes', not 'father'. Recipient word must not leak into query.

scenarios.name: Budget-first — Rs 4000 for mom

turns.content: I have around 4000 rupees to spend on something for my mom.

expect.notes: Budget-first buyers get results immediately, not a form — recipient is already known (mom) so there's nothing left to ask before acting. (maxPrice/budget field name differs between searchGifts and optimizeBudget, so not asserted directly.)

scenarios.name: Recipient-led — younger brother turning 20

turns.content: Need something for my younger brother, he's turning 20 next week.

expect.notes: Birthday occasion known — search first, ask a lean-in question after cards, not before.

scenarios.name: Recipient known, then becomes uncertain — must open picker on turn 2

turns.content: something for my dad
[PLACEHOLDER — dad gift turn]
actually you pick, whatever's good

expect.notes: Even though the recipient was already known from turn 1, once the buyer explicitly hands off the decision ('you pick') on a later turn, the picker must open rather than the model guessing another category.

scenarios.name: 'Suggest me' with no prior context — must not stall silently

turns.content: hi there
[MODE: CHAT] Hey! What are we shopping for today?
suggest me something

expect.notes: REGRESSION-STYLE TEST: in production this exact pattern (a bare 'suggest me' with zero recipient/occasion context) produced a turn with no tool call at all — the agent just talked. Must take SOME concrete action (search a sensible default or open the picker), never stall with text only.
